domain: Function Calling & Tool Use Training for SLMs

gaps.title: End-to-End SLM Function Calling Toolkit (Data Gen → Train → Eval → Deploy)
gaps.description: No single tool covers the full pipeline: synthetic data generation for custom APIs, fine-tuning with LoRA/QLoRA, evaluation on BFCL/ToolBench, and export to ONNX/GGUF for edge deployment. Users must stitch together APIGen + LlamaFactory + BFCL + llama.cpp manually.
gaps.evidence: Landscape shows 4+ separate tools needed. Microsoft guide for Phi-3 function calling requires Azure ML + custom scripts + manual evaluation.
gaps.target_users: ML engineers building on-device/edge AI agents

gaps.title: Sub-1B Function Calling Models with Multi-Turn + Parallel Calling
gaps.description: xLAM-1b exists but lacks multi-turn support. Octopus-2B is on-device but only supports single Android API calls. No sub-1B model handles multi-turn conversations with parallel function calling — critical for IoT, wearables, mobile agents.
gaps.evidence: Feature matrix shows no tool with sub_3B + multi_turn + parallel_function_calling all true. xLAM-1b only does single-turn. Octopus is Android-only.
gaps.target_users: Edge AI developers, mobile app developers, IoT companies

gaps.title: Custom Enterprise API Dataset Generator (No-Code)
gaps.description: APIGen requires coding expertise and custom API implementations. No tool lets enterprise teams upload OpenAPI/Swagger specs and auto-generate function calling training datasets with verification. Enterprises need this to fine-tune SLMs on their internal APIs.
gaps.evidence: APIGen pipeline requires Python, executable APIs, and ML expertise. Microsoft guide requires 80-100 manual examples. No no-code solution exists.
gaps.target_users: Enterprise ML teams, DevOps teams, product managers

gaps.title: RL/GRPO-Based Training for Function Calling SLMs
gaps.description: Current function calling training is almost entirely SFT-based. No open-source tool applies GRPO, PPO, or reward model-based RL specifically to function calling quality in SLMs. OpenAI offers RFT but only for their models. Fireworks offers it but as a managed service.
gaps.evidence: All open-source models (xLAM, Hammer, ToolACE, NexusRaven) use SFT only. ToolRM paper exists but no open implementation for SLMs. ToolSample paper proposes curriculum RL but is research-only.
gaps.target_users: ML researchers, teams needing higher accuracy than SFT provides

gaps.title: Domain-Specific Function Calling Benchmarks (Healthcare, Finance, Legal, Auto)
gaps.description: BFCL and ToolBench evaluate generic API calling. No benchmark exists for domain-specific function calling (EHR/FHIR APIs, financial trading APIs, legal document APIs, automotive CAN bus). The automotive paper is isolated research.
gaps.evidence: BFCL V4 covers generic Python/JS/Java/REST. Automotive paper exists but no reusable benchmark. No healthcare, finance, or legal function calling benchmark.
gaps.target_users: Regulated industry ML teams, vertical SaaS companies

gaps.title: Function Calling with Reasoning/CoT for SLMs
gaps.description: Only NexusRaven and ToolLLaMA include chain-of-thought reasoning before function calls. No sub-7B model combines reasoning with function calling. This is critical for complex multi-step tool use where the model must plan which tools to call and in what order.
gaps.evidence: Feature matrix: reasoning_chain_of_thought is rare. DeepSeek-R1 has reasoning but no function calling focus. No reasoning + function calling dataset for SLMs.
existing_partial_solutions: NexusRaven V2 (13B, too large for edge)
gaps.target_users: Developers building complex multi-step agent workflows on edge

gaps.title: Cross-Platform On-Device Function Calling (iOS + Android + Web + Desktop)
gaps.description: Octopus is Android-only. No SLM is trained for cross-platform function calling covering iOS (Shortcuts/Intents), Android (Intents), Web (Browser APIs), and Desktop (OS APIs). Google's on-device SLM work is Gemini Nano-specific.
gaps.evidence: Octopus V2 trained specifically on Android APIs. Google Edge AI blog covers Gemini Nano only. No cross-platform training data or model.
gaps.target_users: Cross-platform app developers, companies with multi-platform products

gaps.title: Streaming/Incremental Function Call Generation for SLMs
gaps.description: No SLM training approach addresses streaming function calls — generating partial JSON that can be validated and executed incrementally. This is needed for low-latency UX where users see tool call progress in real-time.
gaps.evidence: All current training produces complete function calls. OpenAI supports streaming tool calls at inference but no training methodology exists for optimizing this.
existing_partial_solutions: OpenAI streaming tool calls (inference only, no training method)

gaps.title: Distillation-Free Training Pipeline (No GPT-4 Dependency)
gaps.description: Most datasets (ToolBench, Glaive, ToolACE) use GPT-4 for data generation, creating legal/licensing risks. Only NexusRaven avoids this. No turnkey pipeline generates high-quality function calling data using only open-source models.
gaps.evidence: NexusRaven is the only commercial-permissive, GPT-4-free approach. APIGen uses GPT-4 for generation. ToolBench uses ChatGPT for annotations.
gaps.target_users: Companies with strict IP/licensing requirements, open-source purists

gaps.title: Visual/GUI Function Calling Training for SLMs
gaps.description: Fara-7B explores computer use but at 7B. No sub-3B model is trained for visual/GUI function calling — clicking buttons, filling forms, navigating UIs. This is needed for RPA and browser automation on edge.
gaps.evidence: Fara-7B is Microsoft research, 7B params. Octopus is text-only API calling. No multimodal + function calling training for sub-3B.

gaps.title: Continual Learning / Online Adaptation for Function Calling
gaps.description: APIs change frequently (new endpoints, deprecated params). No approach supports continual learning or online fine-tuning for function calling SLMs without catastrophic forgetting. Models must be retrained from scratch.
gaps.evidence: Gorilla mentions adapting to document changes via retrieval but no continual learning. All models are static post-training.

gaps.title: Function Calling Safety & Guardrails Training
gaps.description: No training approach specifically addresses safety guardrails for function calling — preventing dangerous API calls (delete operations, financial transactions, PII exposure) without explicit user confirmation.
gaps.evidence: No dataset or training method includes safety-aware function calling. All models optimize for accuracy, not safety.
existing_partial_solutions: When2Call paper (when not to call, but not safety-focused)

gaps.title: Unified HuggingFace-Compatible Function Calling Data Format
gaps.description: Every dataset uses a different format: xLAM uses JSON with 'query/answers', Glaive uses system/user/assistant turns, ToolBench uses instruction/solution paths, ToolACE uses its own multi-agent format. No standardized format exists.
gaps.evidence: Axolotl GitHub discussion #1353 asks 'How to use Function Calling datasets' — users struggle with format conversion. LlamaFactory has FunctionFormatter but it's framework-specific.
gaps.target_users: ML engineers who want to combine datasets

gaps.title: Cost-Effective Function Calling Fine-Tuning for Startups
gaps.description: OpenAI fine-tuning is pay-per-token. Fireworks is managed. Self-hosting requires GPUs. No solution offers affordable, serverless function calling fine-tuning specifically optimized for SLMs on free/cheap compute (Colab, Kaggle, CPU-only).
gaps.evidence: Microsoft guide uses Azure ML. Unsloth reduces memory but still needs GPU. No optimized pipeline for Colab free tier or CPU-only environments.
existing_partial_solutions: Unsloth (2x faster but still needs GPU)
Google Colab free tier (limited GPU time)

gaps.title: Multi-Agent Tool Coordination Training for SLMs
gaps.description: Current training focuses on single-agent tool use. No dataset or training method addresses multi-agent scenarios where multiple SLMs coordinate tool calls, handle conflicts, and share tool state.
gaps.evidence: ToolMind uses multi-agent for data generation but trains single-agent models. No multi-agent tool coordination training exists.

key_themes: End-to-end tooling is fragmented — no unified pipeline
Sub-3B models lack advanced capabilities (multi-turn, parallel, reasoning)
On-device/edge deployment is underserved beyond Octopus/Hammer
Domain-specific and enterprise use cases have no dedicated solutions
Safety, continual learning, and multi-agent coordination are unexplored
